meaning: adj.不激动的,不兴奋的,镇静的,镇定的
examples: This stimulated emission is the opposite of (stimulated) absorption, where unexcited matter is stimulated into an excited state by a light wave.
Well, you should be absolutely unexcited because it doesn't mean anything; if they were giving you extra shares and just you alone, that would be great — that would be good.
At extremely cold temperatures, it goes quantum: it exists in an oscillating waveform spanning an excited state, an unexcited state, or both simultaneously, all controlled by electrical currents.
When an electron drops back from an excited to an unexcited state, it gives out light.
They bring with them a fixed idea of what a university is all about, and are unexcited by what they consider their chancellor's unglamorous goal.
